owasp_category: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
title: Exfiltrate Data With No Alerting In Place
objective: Exfiltrate the customer database backup without triggering any security alert.
briefing: Recon shows the audit-logging/alerting service on this host is stopped. Plain web access logs still run, but nothing is watching for suspicious activity.
hints: Check what's running on this host - is anything actually watching for suspicious behavior?
With the audit/alerting service down, a large download won't raise any flags - it'll just look like ordinary traffic.
debrief: The download succeeded and even showed up in the ordinary web access log - but because the audit-logging/alerting service was stopped, nothing flagged a multi-megabyte download of a customer database backup as suspicious. This is a Security Logging and Monitoring Failure (OWASP A09:2021): logs existing isn't the same as anything being monitored or alerted on. The fix is centralized logging with active alerting rules for anomalous behavior (large transfers, off-hours access, repeated failures) and regularly testing that detection actually fires - not just confirming logs are being written somewhere.

logs: [WARN] audit-logging service stopped - no security events will be recorded
